goal: Add multiple identity grooves so different trajectories can be learned, separated, routed, and continued without contaminating one another.

core_principle.identity: stable sequence of local -(i) / O* selections plus phase-flow signature
core_principle.groove: reinforced continuation corridor for one identity
core_principle.router: selects the active groove for the current trajectory

routing_logic.select_existing: choose highest scoring groove if score >= create_threshold
routing_logic.create_new: if no groove score passes threshold and max_grooves not reached
routing_logic.switch_rule: switch only if new groove exceeds current groove by switch_margin

native_platform/run_native_platform.py: after op_star and phi_oriented are computed: active_groove = router.route(phi_oriented, op_star)
get groove feedback vector from router.active_feedback_vector()
pass groove feedback into continuation engine or blend with trace feedback
native_platform/residue_phase_continuati: if external_feedback_vec is provided, apply it as identity-specific groove feedback

continuation_rule.combined: phi_continued = normalize(local + beta_trace * trace_vec + beta_groove * groove_vec)

tests.name: repeat_A_reuses_groove
tests.expect: replay_A routes to same groove as A

tests.name: contrast_B_new_groove
tests.expect: B creates or routes to a different groove

tests.name: no_contamination
tests.expect: B does not reinforce A groove

tests.name: return_to_A
tests.expect: A after B returns to original A groove

success_criteria: A has stable active_groove_id across repeated passes
replay_A continuation_mismatch lower than first A exposure
A groove does not grow during B
failure_conditions: every run creates a new groove
all signals route to one groove
B lowers mismatch by riding A groove
final_instruction: Implement multi-groove routing as identity-specific continuation memory. Do not replace local -(i) selection; use O* and phase signature to route grooves.
